title: nginx returning 504 timeouts under load
description: Health checks began failing around 14:20 UTC. Requests were timing out after 30s. No recent config changes were logged.
resolution: Restarted nginx, which cleared the timeouts. Root cause traced to a stuck connection pool from a downstream dependency. Added a connection pool timeout in the next release.

title: nginx unresponsive after deploy
description: Users reported the app hanging on load. Health endpoint returned 503. A deploy had gone out 10 minutes earlier.
resolution: Rolled back the deploy, which restored service within 2 minutes. Root cause was a missing environment variable in the new build that caused nginx to fail silently on startup.

title: database disk usage hit 95%
description: Disk space alert fired for the database volume. Writes began failing intermittently once usage crossed 95%.
resolution: Cleared old WAL archive files and expanded the volume by 50GB. Recommended setting up automated archive cleanup going forward.

title: database disk full, writes rejected
description: Application errors began appearing referencing 'no space left on device'. Disk usage was at 99%.
resolution: Deleted rotated log files older than 30 days and archived a large unused table to cold storage, which freed enough space to restore normal operation.

title: auth-service CPU pegged at 100%
description: CPU usage on auth-service climbed to 100% and stayed there for over an hour. The login queue backed up significantly.
resolution: Identified a runaway job stuck in a retry loop due to a malformed token payload. Killed the job and added payload validation before allowing retries.

title: auth-service high CPU and slow login processing
description: CPU usage stayed around 91% for several hours, slowing login processing noticeably. No single request appeared to be stuck.
resolution: Traced to an inefficient token-validation query added in a recent release that was being called far more often than expected. Reverted the change and cached validation results instead.

title: auth-service degraded and unresponsive with elevated CPU
description: auth-service began responding slowly and intermittently timing out while CPU usage was also elevated around 90%. It was unclear whether the CPU spike caused the degraded responses or the reverse.
resolution: Restarted the service, which resolved both symptoms simultaneously. The post-mortem suggested a memory leak was driving up CPU usage until the process became degraded and unresponsive.
